arts.ttl: Friday’s briefing: Man Utd, Tottenham and Rangers secure Europa League progress
arts.sum: Bruno Fernandes’ hat-trick helped United beat Real Sociedad 4-1, while Friday will see new England boss Thomas Tuchel name his first squad.

arts.ttl: Listen: Reaction as Rangers reach Europa League last eight
arts.sum: Post-match reaction as Rangers win penalty shootout to reach Europa League last eight.

arts.ttl: Rangers show 'character in abundance' to defy Ibrox woes
arts.sum: Barry Ferguson hails Rangers' "character" as they keep their "special" Europa League campaign going by knocking out Fenerbahce on penalties despite their Ibrox woes continuing.

arts.ttl: Jose Mourinho hits out at ‘arrogant’ referee over Fenerbahce loss to Rangers
arts.sum: The Portuguese boss was left upset after claiming his side deserved three penalties over 120 minutes before the Scottish side triumphed on penalties

arts.ttl: Barry Ferguson takes Rangers ownership of HIS team as he delivers passionate address after nerve-wracking shootout win
arts.sum: In his biggest week in the job, the interim boss has guided his team into the quarter finals of the Europa League

arts.ttl: Furious Jose Mourinho claims Fenerbache denied THREE penalties vs Rangers as he airs astonishing conspiracy claim
arts.sum: The Special One raised fresh fears Fenerbahce are paying for his own indiscretions

arts.ttl: Rangers takeover could be completed by May - gossip
arts.sum: Rangers takeover nears completion as Scotland defenders face injury worries.

arts.ttl: Ally McCoist spots 'unbelievable' unseen Jose Mourinho moment with Rangers fans at final whistle
arts.sum: The Special One watched Fenerbahce crash out of the Europa League on penalties at Ibrox

arts.ttl: Butland’s shootout heroics sink Fenerbahce and seal Rangers progress
arts.sum: There was always the sense that, despite the two-goal advantage Rangers had amassed in Istanbul, the second leg of this tie would not pass quietly. It finally ended with Barry Ferguson’s players bouncing up and down in front of the Copland Road stand while José Mourinho, who had worn a face as ashen as his all-grey outfit as Fenerbahce fluffed their lines from the spot, led his to perform mea culpas 100 metres away. The night had swung in multiple directions but, over their week’s work, Rangers deserved their shot at toppling Athletic Bilbao in the last eight. Mourinho could only watch as Fenerbahce, having levelled the tie up and brought on the cavalry, failed to put Rangers away in the final 50 minutes. He would surely have expected more from Dusan Tadic and Fred, both of whom saw Jack Butland make tremendous saves, in the shoot-out; when Mert Hakan Yandas spooned the final penalty over it was party time for the home support, a miss from Ianis Hagi amongst it all rendered entirely irrelevant. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Butland penalty hero as Rangers progress in EL despite Fenerbahce defeat
arts.sum: Jack Butland was Rangers' penalty hero as they beat Fenerbahce to reach the Europa League quarter-finals on a tense night at Ibrox.
